metadata.summary: In August 2023, auditors from the Ministry of Corporate Affairs flagged severe irregularities in the financial statements of VedaCorp Solutions Pvt. Ltd., a mid-sized IT services firm based in Navi Mumbai. The company reported a 47% year-over-year revenue surge and a net profit of ₹18.2 crore, despite industry-wide slowdowns and declining client retention. Internal whistleblower complaints led to a forensic audit, revealing that ₹12.7 crore in revenue was fabricated through shell companies linked to directors and their relatives. The audit also uncovered forged board meeting minutes approving non-existent vendor contracts and altered bank transaction logs. Shareholders, including a retired government officer and a family trust, filed a petition alleging misappropriation and breach of fiduciary duty. The case escalated when two key witnesses — the CFO and the external auditor — retracted their initial statements, claiming coercion. The company’s founder, Arjun Mehta, denies all allegations, pointing to a disgruntled ex-employee as the source of the leak. Meanwhile, a mysterious ₹5 crore transfer from VedaCorp’s payroll account to a dormant account under the name 'Sahara Ventures' has gone unexplained, despite being flagged by the bank’s AI fraud detection system. Regulatory authorities suspect a multi-layered scheme involving forged invoices, bribed auditors, and a complex web of shell entities to launder funds and inflate equity value ahead of a planned IPO.

hidden_ground_truth.true_motive: To inflate VedaCorp’s valuation to secure a ₹250 crore private equity investment and exit before the fraud was discovered, while diverting ₹18 crore to offshore accounts under family trusts.
true_timeline: 2022-11-03: Arjun Mehta instructs CFO Rajiv Desai to create fake vendor contracts with shell companies controlled by his wife, Priya Mehta.
2022-12-15: Dr. Anjali Kulkarni, a board member and external auditor, is bribed with ₹15 lakh to approve falsified financials without independent verification.
2023-01-20: ₹8 crore is routed through Sahara Ventures (shell entity) to a Cyprus-based trust via three intermediary accounts.
2023-03-10: Forged board minutes are created to retroactively approve the vendor contracts, signed by a digitally cloned signature of independent director Mr. Ravi Nair (who was on leave).
2023-05-12: Ex-employee Rohan Verma is terminated after raising concerns internally; he later files a whistleblower complaint.
2023-07-28: Priya Mehta withdraws ₹3.2 crore from Sahara Ventures into her personal account under the guise of 'dividend income'.
2023-08-10: External auditor firm 'Spectrum Audit' issues a clean opinion based on falsified documents provided by Rajiv Desai.
2023-08-15: MCA audit triggers investigation; Rajiv Desai and Dr. Kulkarni are approached by CBI for questioning but are bribed to retract testimony.
2023-08-20: Rajiv Desai files a false affidavit claiming Rohan Verma planted the fraud to sabotage the IPO.
2023-08-25: Dr. Kulkarni recants her initial statement, claiming she was pressured by Arjun Mehta to sign off — but later retracts again, saying she was threatened by 'unknown persons'.
forged_evidence: Board meeting minutes dated 2023-03-10 with digitally forged signature of Mr. Ravi Nair
Invoices from 'Sahara Ventures' and 'Nirvana Tech Solutions' with altered GSTINs and non-existent company registrations
Bank transaction logs from Axis Bank showing 'Sahara Ventures' as a legitimate vendor — later proven to be manipulated by internal IT staff under Rajiv Desai's orders
Email thread from Priya Mehta to Arjun Mehta titled 'Dividend Distribution Plan' — timestamp backdated to 2022-10-15 to appear before the fraud began

injected_contradictions.description: Rajiv Desai claims in his first affidavit that he acted alone under Arjun Mehta’s orders, but later testifies that Dr. Kulkarni initiated the fraud scheme.

Dr. Kulkarni’s initial statement says she was unaware of the shell companies, but her personal email shows she received a ₹15 lakh payment from Sahara Ventures on 2023-01-22 — which she denies ever receiving.
Rohan Verma’s whistleblower email claims he saw Arjun Mehta sign the forged minutes, but the digital metadata of the email shows it was sent from a device logged into Priya Mehta’s account.
The external auditor’s report states that all vendor entities were verified via physical site visits — yet CCTV footage from the addresses listed shows no such visits occurred.
Arjun Mehta claims the ₹5 crore transfer to Sahara Ventures was a 'loan repayment' from a client, but no client named Sahara Ventures exists in VedaCorp’s client database.
Priya Mehta claims she never owned Sahara Ventures, but the MCA registry shows her as the sole director appointed in 2022 — a filing she denies authorizing.

contradictions.claim_a: Rajiv Desai claims the ₹5 crore transfer to Sahara Ventures was a legitimate client repayment for services rendered in Q4 2022.
contradictions.claim_b: VedaCorp’s client ledger shows no client named Sahara Ventures, and no contract or invoice exists for any service rendered to them in Q4 2022.

contradictions.claim_a: Dr. Anjali Kulkarni initially testified that she never received any payment from Sahara Ventures.
contradictions.claim_b: Bank records show a ₹15 lakh credit to her personal account from Sahara Ventures on 2023-01-22, with a memo 'Audit Fee - Q4 2022'.

contradictions.claim_a: The board meeting minutes of 2023-03-10 bear the digital signature of independent director Ravi Nair.
contradictions.claim_b: Ravi Nair was on medical leave in Goa from 2023-02-28 to 2023-03-18 and never accessed VedaCorp’s digital signing system during that period.

contradictions.claim_a: Rohan Verma claims he discovered the fraud on 2023-06-10 and reported it immediately to HR.
contradictions.claim_b: HR email logs show his complaint was filed on 2023-07-03, and he was terminated on 2023-07-05 — two days after the first internal audit flagged anomalies.

contradictions.claim_a: The audited financials show ₹18.2 crore net profit, supported by ₹42 crore in revenue from 12 vendors.
contradictions.claim_b: Only 3 of the 12 vendors are registered with the GSTN; the rest have invalid or suspended registrations, and their bank accounts were opened after the alleged transactions.

contradictions.claim_a: Priya Mehta claims she had no knowledge of Sahara Ventures and never signed any documents related to it.
contradictions.claim_b: MCA filings show her Aadhaar-linked digital signature was used to register Sahara Ventures on 2022-11-12 — the same day she was in Delhi attending a family wedding, per flight records.

contradictions.claim_a: An email from Arjun Mehta to Rajiv Desai dated 2023-01-05 says, 'Make sure Kulkarni gets her bonus — she’s doing great work.'
contradictions.claim_b: The email server logs show this email was sent from Priya Mehta’s device, not Arjun Mehta’s, and the IP address matches the company’s internal network during off-hours.

contradictions.claim_a: The external auditor’s report states that all vendor payments were verified via bank reconciliation.
contradictions.claim_b: The bank reconciliation report submitted to the auditor was manually edited in Excel — metadata shows the original file was overwritten on 2023-07-18, after the audit began.

contradictions.claim_a: Arjun Mehta claims he was in Singapore on business from 2023-03-01 to 2023-03-15 and could not have approved the forged minutes.
contradictions.claim_b: His passport shows he returned on 2023-03-10, and airport CCTV shows him entering the VedaCorp office at 10:45 PM that same night — the same time the minutes were digitally signed.

contradictions.claim_a: Sahara Ventures is listed as a private limited company with 100% ownership by Priya Mehta.
contradictions.claim_b: The company’s PAN and GSTIN were registered using a duplicate Aadhaar card of Dr. Anjali Kulkarni — which she claims was stolen in 2021 but never reported.

nodes.label: Email: 'Make sure Kulkarni gets her bonus'
